title: Teaching Inside the Thread

step1.heading: Step 1. What is your teaching goal today?
step1.subhead: Pick the area that best matches what you want to work on. You can refine the focus on the next screen.

categories.label: Course design and architecture
categories.description: AI-aware syllabi, curriculum mapping, and course planning at the architectural level.

categories.label: Teaching technical content
categories.description: Databases, systems analysis, programming, analytics, and ML pedagogy with AI in the room.

categories.label: Cases, examples, and applications
categories.description: Case-method teaching, discipline-specific examples, and applied scenarios for IS classrooms.

categories.label: Engagement and interaction
categories.description: Discussion design, in-class AI activities, and student and AI exchange protocols.

categories.label: Assessment in the AI era
categories.description: Assignment design under AI access, rubric revision, and integrity strategy.

categories.label: Doctoral training and supervision
categories.description: Qualifying exams, dissertation work, research methods training, and mentorship with AI.

categories.label: Building learning artifacts
categories.description: Custom GPTs for class, AI study companions, and agentic tools students can use.

categories.label: Other
categories.description: Something else. Describe it on the next screen.

step2.heading: Step 2. What is the specific focus?
step2.subhead: Pick a focus area to narrow the conversation. Real options arrive in the next content cycle.

course_design.label: Sample focus area for Course design and architecture (placeholder)

technical_content.label: Sample focus area for Teaching technical content (placeholder)

cases_examples.label: Sample focus area for Cases, examples, and applications (placeholder)

engagement.label: Sample focus area for Engagement and interaction (placeholder)

assessment.label: Sample focus area for Assessment in the AI era (placeholder)

doctoral.label: Sample focus area for Doctoral training and supervision (placeholder)

artifacts.label: Sample focus area for Building learning artifacts (placeholder)

step3.heading: Step 3. Teaching context
step3.subhead: A few details so the prompt fits your situation.

fields.label: Course topic
fields.placeholder: For example: Database Management, IS Strategy, ML for Business

fields.label: Current approach (optional)
fields.placeholder: How are you currently teaching this, if at all? If you are designing from scratch, leave blank.

step4.heading: Step 4. What materials can you build on?
step4.subhead: Pick anything you have available. You can also add your own.

review.heading: Review and continue
review.subhead: Confirm your selections. The Companion will copy your prompt and open the agent in a new tab.
review.primary_button: Copy prompt and open the Companion

bridge.heading: The agent is opening
bridge.confirmation: Your prompt is on your clipboard. The AMCIS Teaching Companion should have opened in a new tab.
bridge.m_open_framing: This prompt is your M-Open, the start of a multi-turn exchange. When you finish, you can paste an M-Close into the thread or close the tab.

reflection.heading: Reflection
reflection.subhead: Three small blocks. Each one is short. None are required.

r_block.prompt: Share a short reflection on what changed for you.
r_block.placeholder: A few sentences. What shifted, what you noticed, what you might try next.

q_block.prompt: Which of these AI-in-teaching practices feels most likely to change something about your teaching this fall?

o_block.prompt: Rank these dimensions of AI-assisted teaching by how much they concern you.

next_actions.try_x: Try an X block, open exploration

x_block.heading: Exploration block
x_block.orienting_text: If you have time, try an X block. Open a free exploration with the agent. Type ### X as your first line and ask anything the workshop did not address. The thread captures it. We do not grade it.
x_block.reopen_agent_label: Re-open the Companion in a new tab
